memory_candidates.content: The OpenCode TUI embedded HTTP server (started by opencode --port) exposes a Server-Sent Events endpoint at GET /event with no authentication required. Events use standard SSE format. Key assistant-response events: message.part.updated (streaming text), message.updated (role=assistant), and session.idle (clean completion signal). Pure aiohttp can consume the stream via async iteration over response.content.

When implementing a bidirectional relay where one path uses blocking POST and another uses SSE subscription, deduplication is required to prevent double-posting. Pattern: maintain a set of in-progress session IDs; add session_id before relay, remove after response is posted; SSE subscriber checks the set before posting.

artifacts.summary: Full analysis of OpenCode SSE event API, current bot architecture, deduplication strategy, and concrete implementation plan for TUI->Discord relay via TuiSseSubscriber class
Implementation plan for bidirectional Discord-OpenCode relay with TuiSseSubscriber, deduplication, and integration hooks

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode and Claude Code have incompatible agent frontmatter schemas for the tools field. Claude Code uses YAML arrays (- Read, - Write, etc.) while OpenCode expects tools as a record/object or absent entirely. When porting agent .md files from .claude/agents/ to .opencode/agent/subagents/, the tools field must be stripped or converted. OpenCode auto-scans subagents/ on startup and crashes with expected record, received array if it encounters a YAML array tools field.

OpenCode runtime crashes on startup if any .md file in .opencode/agent/subagents/ contains a tools field as a YAML array. The runtime auto-scans all subagent files and expects tools as an object format or omitted entirely. Claude Code agents use tools as YAML arrays of capitalized names. When porting agent definitions between systems, the tools field must be stripped or converted.

Implementation plan with 2 phases covering frontmatter documentation update, opencode.lua commit, and verification
Implementation summary for frontmatter documentation update and cross-system porting guide

memory_candidates.content: Status transition enforcement in the agent system is entirely documentation-driven (Claude-facing markdown instructions), not programmatic. The update-task-status.sh script performs no transition validation -- it only does idempotency checks and atomic file updates. All enforcement comes from Allowed Statuses tables in skill-orchestrator, checkpoint-gate-in, and per-command GATE IN sections.

When simplifying enforcement rules across a large markdown-based agent system, the most effective pattern is: (1) identify the canonical implementation first (skill-planner already had the permissive model), (2) use it as a template for all other files, (3) copy parent files to extension core mirrors last using cp + diff verification.

memory_candidates.content: CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 only triggers fork when subagent_type is OMITTED from the Agent/Task tool call. Skills that explicitly specify subagent_type are unaffected by this env var.

context: fork (skill frontmatter) and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 (env var) solve opposite problems. context: fork creates isolation, while FORK_SUBAGENT creates inheritance. They are complementary, not alternatives.

completed_projects.description: Fix phase status marker updates across implementation agents in the Vision repository. Three issues to address: (1) grant-agent lacks Phase Checkpoint Protocol - does not update plan-level or phase-level status markers ([NOT STARTED] -> [IN PROGRESS] -> [COMPLETED]) during the assemble workflow, unlike general-implementation-agent which has Stage 4A (mark phase in progress) and Stage 4D (mark phase complete) with per-phase git commits; (2) skill-grant lacks calls to update-plan-status.sh at preflight and postflight, unlike skill-implementer which calls this script; (3) latex-implementation-agent and typst-implementation-agent have incomplete phase marker instructions - they mention updates but lack detailed Edit tool patterns and a Phase Checkpoint Protocol section. Fix all three by: adding a Phase Checkpoint Protocol section to grant-agent mirroring general-implementation-agent, adding update-plan-status.sh calls to skill-grant, and expanding phase marker instructions in latex/typst implementation agents. All agents should update TODO.md, state.json, plan file phase headers, and plan metadata consistently.

completed_projects.description: Create financial-analysis.typ template at .claude/extensions/founder/context/project/founder/templates/typst/financial-analysis.typ following cost-breakdown.typ pattern. Model sections after templates/financial-analysis.md but all numerical values must come from JSON data, not hardcoded.

completed_projects.description: Fix link-artifact-todo.sh: when /research inserts a Research link, it passes next_field="**Plan**" as the insertion anchor. On tasks that have never been planned, no **Plan** line exists in TODO.md, so the script fails with "could not find insertion point". The error message mentions **Description** as a fallback but the code never actually searches for it. Add a fallback chain so that when the primary next_field is not found, the script tries **Description** (which always exists). /plan and /implement are not affected since they already use next_field="**Description**".

memory_candidates.content: When creating design-partner agents (as opposed to critic/review agents), use a translation model workflow: understand intent -> translate to professional perspective -> identify divergence -> reframe -> validate consistency. The key distinction is collaborative posture (assume the content is right but expressed in wrong vocabulary) vs adversarial posture (assume errors exist). This applies to any domain where users need to communicate with professionals who have specialized vocabulary.

memory_candidates.content: The extension loader (shared/extensions/init.lua) manager.load() ignores the manifest dependencies field entirely. Dependency resolution should be inserted between the is_loaded check (line 244) and the conflict check (line 249). The loading stack pattern via opts._loading_stack enables circular dependency detection through recursion. The picker (picker/init.lua) is a thin UI layer that needs no changes -- it delegates to manager.load() which is the correct injection point.

Extension dependency auto-loading pattern: In manager.load(), resolve dependencies between the is_loaded check and the confirmation dialog. Use opts._loading_stack (array of extension names) for circular detection by checking if the current extension already appears in the stack. Pass the stack down through recursive calls. Set a depth limit of 5. Dependencies load with confirm=false (silently). If any dependency fails, the parent fails before copying files.

artifacts.summary: Research on model not found opus/ error in opencode agent system
Implementation plan with 3 phases to remove invalid model: frontmatter from 34 .opencode/ files
Removed model: frontmatter from 34 .opencode/ files and updated documentation to clarify model: is not supported in OpenCode

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode CLI (v1.14.33) uses parseModel() which splits model strings by "/" into providerID and modelID. Model references must be in "provider/model" format (e.g., "opencode/claude-opus-4-7"). Short aliases like "opus" or "sonnet" (valid in Claude Code) cause "Model not found: opus/" errors because they parse as providerID="opus", modelID="". Command frontmatter "model:" has highest priority in model resolution, overriding session model.

OpenCode model resolution order for slash commands: (1) command frontmatter model field, (2) agent configured model, (3) client session model from TUI, (4) session default. This means any invalid model in frontmatter will always override a valid session model and cause errors immediately.

OpenCode's parseModel() splits model strings by '/' separator. Bare aliases like 'opus' produce {providerID: 'opus', modelID: ''} causing 'Model not found: opus/' errors. When porting .claude/ to .opencode/, always remove the model: frontmatter line entirely. OpenCode uses the session model from the TUI picker instead of per-file model declarations.

Fixed incorrect package manager (npx -> uvx). Verified server connects successfully.

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode and Claude Code use completely different MCP server configuration formats. Claude Code uses mcpServers key in settings.json with {command, args} objects. OpenCode uses mcp key in opencode.json with {type: local, command: [array]} objects. Tool naming also differs: Claude Code uses mcp__servername__toolname double-underscore convention, OpenCode uses servername_toolname single-underscore.

OpenCode MCP tool naming convention: tools are named as 'servername_toolname' (single underscore separator), defined in Go source as fmt.Sprintf("%s_%s", b.mcpName, b.tool.Name). This differs from Claude Code's 'mcp__servername__toolname' (double underscore with mcp prefix). When configuring OpenCode agents, use 'servername_*' glob patterns for tool enablement, and reference tools as 'servername_toolname' in agent prompts.

The global OpenCode config at ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json is managed by Home Manager via a Nix module at ~/.dotfiles/modules/opencode.nix, sourcing from ~/.dotfiles/config/opencode.json. Edits must be made to the dotfiles source, not the symlinked target, followed by 'home-manager switch --flake . -b backup' to apply changes.

The lean-lsp-mcp package is a Python package installed via uvx (not npm/npx). The correct MCP server command for OpenCode config is ["uvx", "lean-lsp-mcp"], not ["npx", "-y", "lean-lsp-mcp@latest"]. The Claude Code .mcp.json also uses uvx for this server.

memory_candidates.content:
When specifying LLM-driven file insertion operations in agent definitions, always use concrete Edit tool invocations with exact oldString/newString patterns rather than abstract pseudocode functions like insert_after_heading(). The heading-anchored replacement pattern ('oldString: ## Tasks
' -> 'newString: ## Tasks

{batch}
') is the most LLM-proof approach because the heading text is unique in the file. Always include post-insertion re-read verification to catch LLM interpretation errors before they propagate into version-controlled files.

The system has an established Edit tool invocation convention for heading-anchored replacements: the oldString is the heading line with its trailing newline (e.g. '## Tasks\n'), and the newString prepends content immediately after it. This pattern is used by general-implementation-agent for phase markers, by /review for category insertion, and by skill-spawn for dependency updates. New agent definitions should follow this convention rather than inventing abstract pseudocode.

memory_candidates.content: The extension system uses a managed/unmanaged sidecar file pattern (.opencode.json.managed) to distinguish automatically-managed config files from user-customized ones. This pattern should be extended to govern sync overwrite behavior and cleanup scope, not just base template installation.

The key-based tracking pattern for JSON merge/unmerge (storing added keys in state, removing only tracked keys on unmerge) is a reusable design for any JSON file that needs idempotent extension merging. It preserves user additions and handles missing files gracefully.

Agent name conflicts across extensions are currently handled silently (first-loaded wins, second is skipped). The recommended design is to add explicit conflict detection with user warnings, and document that agent names should be unique across the extension ecosystem.

The OpenCode extension system uses a key-based JSON merge tracking pattern for idempotent unmerge: merge tracks added keys in {keys = {...}}, unmerge removes only tracked keys, and state persists across sessions in extensions.json. This pattern is reusable for any JSON file that needs merge/unmerge semantics and could be generalized beyond opencode.json to other config files.

The TODO(541) annotation pattern in Lua files references the design spec path explicitly, making it easy for future agents to find the relevant design decisions. This pattern can be reused for any design task that produces implementation-guiding annotations.

The JSON merge tracking pattern (key-based tracking with {keys = {...}}) is a reusable design for any additive, reversible JSON merging. It was documented in .opencode/context/patterns/json-merge-tracking.md with pseudocode and reusability instructions.

memory_candidates.content: The OpenCode extension loader has fully-implemented merge/unmerge code for opencode.json agent registration (merge_opencode_agents/unmerge_opencode_agents in merge.lua), but ZERO of 17 extension manifests declare merge_targets.opencode_json, making the code path unreachable. This is why extension agents are invisible to the CLI and why manual opencode.json edits cause crashes on unload.

The opencode.json base template at .opencode/templates/opencode.json is never synced to project root because sync.lua excludes it from root files. This means projects start without a valid opencode.json unless one is created manually, contributing to the manual-edit crash pattern.

Pre-merge validation pattern for opencode.json: before writing agent entries, verify all {file:...} references resolve to existing files. The loader copies agent .md files before processing merge targets, so by the time merge_opencode_agents runs, the referenced files should exist. This validation prevents malformed extension manifests from corrupting project opencode.json.

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode has a comprehensive headless/CLI API: 'opencode serve' starts a persistent server with basic auth (OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD), 'opencode run --command' executes named commands programmatically, 'opencode attach' connects to remote servers, and 'opencode session list/delete' manages session lifecycle. The --format json flag enables machine-parseable output. This client-server architecture means OpenCode agents can be controlled from external services like Discord bots or webhooks without the Neovim TUI.

For Discord bot development on NixOS, nextcord (python3Packages.nextcord, v3.1.1) is the optimal choice: actively maintained (Aug 2025), slash-command-native with decorator-based API, async/aiohttp-based, and available directly in nixpkgs without flake inputs. Prefer nextcord over discord.py for new projects due to more frequent updates and better Python 3.12+ support.

completed_projects.description: Research best practices for Claude Code 2026 hook architectures, lifecycle-aware notification systems, and signal-based event patterns for reducing TTS/WezTerm notification spam

artifacts.summary: B+A Hybrid approach: direct invocation from postflight scripts + signal file for Stop hook gating. Evaluates 4 architectures, documents Claude Code 2026 hook event model, WezTerm OSC 1337 limitations.
4-phase B+A Hybrid plan: core signal infrastructure, lifecycle TTS gating, WezTerm multi-state indicators, documentation update
B+A Hybrid lifecycle notification system: 4 phases implemented (tts-notify.sh lifecycle flag, update-task-status.sh integration, WezTerm multi-state indicators, documentation)

memory_candidates.content: Claude Code 2026 Stop hook stdin JSON includes stop_reason, last_assistant_message, agent_id, agent_type, stop_hook_active, and effort fields. The last_assistant_message field contains the full text of Claude's final response and can be pattern-matched for lifecycle detection. The existing memory-nudge.sh hook already demonstrates this pattern successfully.

WezTerm OSC 1337 user variables are write-only from shell scripts (set via escape sequences to pane TTY). They cannot be read back from bash hooks -- only the WezTerm Lua config (format-tab-title, user-var-changed events) can read them via pane:get_user_vars(). This makes OSC 1337 unsuitable as a primary notification gate in hook scripts, though it works well for visual state indicators.

Claude Code 2026 added TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, TeammateIdle, FileChanged, CwdChanged, WorktreeCreate, and WorktreeRemove hook events beyond the original 12. TaskCreated/TaskCompleted require CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and refer to Claude's internal task concept, not custom task management systems.

The B+A Hybrid notification architecture combines direct invocation (postflight calls tts-notify.sh --lifecycle STATUS) with signal file suppression (Stop hook checks specs/tmp/tts-lifecycle-signal before firing TTS). Signal files older than 60 seconds are treated as stale and ignored. Background execution with & prevents blocking the postflight script.

memory_candidates.content: Claude Code subagent model resolution order: (1) CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL env var, (2) per-invocation model parameter, (3) frontmatter model field, (4) main conversation model. Anthropic official 2026 model selection matrix explicitly recommends Haiku 4.5 for sub-agent tasks and Sonnet 4.6 for code generation, agentic tool use. Claude Code built-in agents use tiered models: Explore uses Haiku, Plan inherits, statusline-setup uses Sonnet. The opusplan alias uses Opus during plan mode and Sonnet for execution.

2026 Claude model pricing: Opus 4.7 = $5/$25 per MTok (input/output), Sonnet 4.6 = $3/$15, Haiku 4.5 = $1/$5. Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer that consumes up to 35% more tokens for the same text. Cache hits are 10% of input price across all models. Sonnet 4.6 scores 79.6% on SWE-bench Verified vs Opus 4.6 80.8% and Opus 4.7 87.6%.

The .claude/ agent system uses a tiered model policy: Opus for deep-reasoning agents (planner-agent, meta-builder-agent, reviser-agent, all formal/lean/math/logic/physics agents, legal-analysis-agent), Sonnet for general-purpose agents (research, implementation, review, spawn, domain-specific agents like neovim/nix/epi/present/latex/founder). Dispatch commands (research, plan, implement) use model: sonnet since the agent model takes precedence. Direct-execution commands keep model: opus. Override flags --opus/--sonnet/--haiku work at invocation time.

artifacts.summary: Root cause analysis of /tmp/state.json.tmp generation: LLM-generated bash commands with 3 inconsistent temp file patterns across 19 files in both nvim and dotfiles .opencode/ directories.

Implementation summary for fixing /tmp/ usage root cause across 26 files in two .opencode/ repositories

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode LLM agents generate /tmp/state.json.tmp when they compose jq atomic-write commands without explicit guidance. Root pattern: two conflicting temp file conventions exist in the agent system - canonical specs/tmp/state.json (used in most skills) vs. in-place specs/state.json.tmp (used in todo.md, review.md, skill-todo, skill-project-overview) and bare state.json.tmp in research-flow-example.md (no specs/ prefix). LLM combines .tmp suffix from examples with /tmp/ from training data. Fix: standardize all to specs/tmp/state.json and add MUST NOT /tmp/ prohibition to AGENTS.md.

In LLM-driven agent systems like OpenCode, documentation IS effectively code. When agents read skill/command files containing code examples with temp file patterns, they replicate those patterns in generated bash commands. Inconsistent patterns across documentation files cause agents to hallucinate hybrid patterns like /tmp/state.json.tmp. Fixing the documentation layer is as important as fixing shell scripts, and explicit prohibition text in the primary context file (AGENTS.md) loaded at session start is the strongest mitigation.

In LLM-driven agent systems (like OpenCode), documentation files ARE effectively code. The LLM reads skill files, command files, and example documents to learn patterns, then generates bash commands based on those patterns. Inconsistent temp file conventions in documentation will cause the LLM to generate ad-hoc combinations that may include system /tmp/ paths. Fix: (1) make all examples consistent, (2) add explicit prohibitions in AGENTS.md loaded at session start, (3) add mkdir -p guards before all temp file writes.

artifacts.summary: Root cause: extension loader overwrites active commands with outdated extension source, stripping COMMAND EXECUTION MODE preamble and routing fixes
4-phase implementation plan: backport commands, add syncprotect to loader, create drift detection script, integration verification
Implementation summary for task 577 covering all 4 phases

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode extension reload (via Neovim extension picker) unconditionally overwrites active command files (.opencode/commands/*.md) with extension source files (.opencode/extensions/core/commands/*.md). Any improvements made directly to active command files are lost on reload. The fix is to keep the extension source in sync with the active files by backporting improvements. The loader.lua copy_file() function has no version checking or drift detection.

The COMMAND EXECUTION MODE preamble in OpenCode commands prevents the LLM from describing commands instead of executing them. All 15 core commands in ~/.config/nvim/.opencode/extensions/core/commands/ are missing this preamble (only in active ~/.config/nvim/.opencode/commands/ files). Extension reload strips the preamble, causing silent describe-instead-of-execute failures.

~/.config/nvim/.opencode/output/ is OpenCode session export directory (created when user presses <leader>x = session_export keybind). Files named after last command invoked. If OpenCode runs with cwd=~/.config/nvim/ rather than intended project directory, session exports and artifact writes target wrong specs/ directory.

The extension loader (loader.lua) and the sync operation (sync.lua) are independent file-copy mechanisms that both write to the same target directories (.claude/ or .opencode/). Improvements made to sync.lua (like .syncprotect support) do not automatically apply to the extension loader. When adding a protection or validation mechanism to one copy path, always check whether the other path needs the same treatment.

When the OpenCode extension loader (loader.lua) copies files via copy_simple_files() and related functions, the relative path format for syncprotect checking uses the category-prefixed form: commands/plan.md, scripts/check-vault-threshold.sh, hooks/log-session.sh, etc.
The extension loader (loader.lua copy_file) now returns (success, skipped) tuple: (true, false) = wrote file, (false, true) = skipped due to syncprotect, (false, false) = write failed.

Command drift between .opencode/commands/ (active) and .opencode/extensions/core/commands/ (source) is a recurring problem. After any manual command improvement, also copy the improved file to the extension source directory to prevent regression on next extension reload. Use check-command-drift.sh to detect drift.

completed_projects.completion_summary: Backported COMMAND EXECUTION MODE preamble and routing fixes to all 17 extension source commands, added .syncprotect awareness to the extension loader so protected files are skipped during reload, and created a drift detection script (check-command-drift.sh) that exits non-zero when active commands diverge from extension source.

memory_candidates.content: opencode.nvim dispatches User autocmds with pattern OpencodeEvent:<type> and event.data structure { event = { type, properties }, port = N }. Any custom autocmd listeners must access event.data.event.properties, not event.data directly. This caused a session tracking bug where session_id was never extracted.

snacks.terminal.get(cmd, opts) with default create behavior is idempotent: it checks terminals[id] first and only calls open() if missing. This makes it safe for server start callbacks. However, get() returns the terminal object (never nil with default opts), so if-not-term guards are dead code.

opencode.events.connected_server caches the last connected server and Server.get() returns it immediately via find(). If the terminal was closed but the cache wasnt cleared, subsequent Server.get() calls return a dead server, causing API failures. Explicit disconnect() before get() mitigates this.

All task 575 fixes remain intact.

memory_candidates.content: OpenCode Glob tool limitation: relative paths starting with .opencode/ (hidden directory prefix) return 0 results. Absolute paths work correctly. Fix: use project_root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) then glob $manifest_dir/*/manifest.json.

OpenCode child project commands (implement.md, research.md, plan.md) are NOT automatically updated when the nvim config versions are fixed. Core system commands in .opencode/commands/ are set up once during project initialization and never automatically synced.

OpenCode Glob tool silently returns zero results for relative paths starting with hidden directories. All manifest discovery code must use absolute paths derived via git rev-parse --show-toplevel. Core routing commands are not managed by the extension loader and require manual propagation to child projects.

OpenCode Glob tool silently returns zero results for relative paths into hidden directories. Fix: derive project root via git rev-parse --show-toplevel then use $project_root/.opencode/extensions for manifest discovery. Use sync-core-commands.sh to propagate to child projects.

When multiple projects share common command infrastructure, use a sync script pattern: one canonical source (nvim .opencode/commands/) and a registry of child project paths. The script supports --dry-run, --check (detect drift), and --commands (select subset). Template: .opencode/scripts/sync-core-commands.sh.

Revised implementation plan incorporating post-571 research findings
Implementation summary for task 571

memory_candidates.content: The discord-bot Neovim integration requires DISCORD_BOT_LINK_TOKEN to be set to any non-empty string in the shell environment, even though the bot side has LINK_API_TOKEN empty (no auth enforced). The Neovim plugin (discord-link.lua) returns an error at line 52 when TOKEN is nil/empty, before making any HTTP request. Set DISCORD_BOT_LINK_TOKEN to any value in home.sessionVariables to enable the integration.

The discord-bot.service has a recurring heartbeat blocking issue: nextcord.gateway warnings indicate the asyncio event loop is stuck, making the aiohttp HTTP API server (port 8080) unresponsive. Fix: systemctl restart discord-bot. The HTTP API is needed for Neovim leader>ar (link) and leader>as (session picker) commands to work.
The OpenCode serve endpoint is fixed at port 4096 (127.0.0.1:4096). The NixOS service config uses --port 4096 explicitly. The bot service has OPENCODE_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:4096 hardcoded.

OpenCode's Glob tool silently returns 0 results for relative paths into hidden directories (e.g., `.opencode/extensions/`). The fix is to derive an absolute path via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` before constructing the manifest search path. This pattern affects all child project command files and was the root cause of task 572's lean routing failure.

The `snacks.terminal.open()` API bypasses dedup logic -- it always creates a new terminal. To prevent duplicate terminal creation, always use `snacks.terminal.get()` with `create = false` first, then conditionally call `open()` only if no terminal exists. This pattern was critical to fixing the OpenCode session picker race condition in tasks 575/576.

OpenCode events dispatched via `opencode.events` use a wrapper structure: `event.data = { event = { type, properties }, port = N }`. The actual properties (e.g., `sessionID`) are nested at `event.data.event.properties`, not at the top level of `event.data`. This caused session tracking to fail silently in task 576.

When writing user-facing documentation for Discord/OpenCode integration, always include both the symptom-and-fix troubleshooting pattern and the last verified date. The combination of research reports, source code reading, and style reference comparison is essential for accurate technical guides.

memory_candidates.content: WezTerm task number hook uses 3-tier UserPromptSubmit logic: (1) Workflow command with task number -> SET TASK_NUMBER to compact spec; (2) Any slash command without task number -> CLEAR TASK_NUMBER; (3) Free text / follow-up answer -> PRESERVE (no change). The preserve tier prevents stale-on-follow-up where answering an agent question during a workflow would clear the task number display.

Multi-task syntax in wezterm-task-number.sh: capture the full task spec with [0-9][0-9, -]* then post-process: strip from first -- (removes flags like --team), trim trailing spaces/commas. Display as compact spec with spaces removed (e.g. 7, 22-24, 59 -> 7,22-24,59).

memory_candidates.content: Signal-file + Stop hook pattern for Claude Code lifecycle notifications: update-task-status.sh writes a status value to .claude/tmp/lifecycle-signal as its last postflight action. A unified claude-stop-notify.sh in the Stop hook reads and atomically consumes the file (using mv rename for race-safe single-consumer semantics). If signal present: announces lifecycle status via wezterm-notify.sh + tts-notify.sh --lifecycle. If not: falls back to needs_input + bare Tab N TTS. This replaces the unreliable Stage 8a markdown-instruction pattern where agents would call lifecycle-notify.sh.

Subagent Stop hook suppression: The Claude Code Stop hook fires for BOTH main agent and subagent turns. To suppress TTS/color updates in subagent contexts, read stdin JSON and check for agent_id field (only present in subagent Stop contexts).

OpenCode wezterm-notify.sh divergence: The .opencode/ copies of wezterm-notify.sh hardcode needs_input instead of accepting a STATUS parameter like the .claude/ version does. Similarly, opencode tts-notify.sh lacks --lifecycle mode. Any lifecycle notification feature must update both trees in parallel.

Signal-file pattern for cross-concern hook communication: when a postflight script needs to communicate state to a later-firing Stop hook, write a signal file as the LAST action (after all state updates), then have the Stop hook atomically consume it via mv-rename.

Dual-dispatch notification architecture: fire TTS and WezTerm from BOTH postflight immediately AND Stop hook conditionally. Signal file as suppress flag prevents duplication. Compatible with never-stopping workflows.

completed_projects.description: Fix Neovim rendering corruption after system sleep in WezTerm. Primary root cause: yanky.nvim system_clipboard.sync_with_ring = true triggers a blocking wl-paste call via FocusGained on wake. On Wayland/GNOME, wl-paste can hang indefinitely when the compositor clipboard state is stale after sleep, freezing the entire Neovim TUI. Solution: replace yanky.nvim with a custom yank ring module (~460 LOC across 6 modules under lua/neotex/yank/) that uses vim.system() with a 2-second timeout for all clipboard reads. Includes post-sleep rendering recovery autocommands (mode, redraw!, treesitter invalidation). Research completed in dotfiles task 59 -- 3 research reports copied over.

artifacts.summary: Root cause analysis: yanky.nvim system_clipboard.sync_with_ring triggers blocking wl-paste on FocusGained after sleep. Secondary: missing VimResume/FocusGained recovery autocommands.
Feature audit of yanky.nvim usage, alternative plugin comparison, custom implementation feasibility (~200 LOC), and non-blocking clipboard sync design via vim.system().
Complete custom yank ring architecture: 6 modules under lua/neotex/yank/ (~460 LOC), production-quality code sketches, plugin spec, and file change list.
Implementation summary: 5 phases, 6 modules created, yanky.nvim replaced, all verified with nvim --headless
Diagnostic report: implementation attempt reverted. Blinking persists even with all visual operations disabled, ruling out our code. Issue is at Neovim/WezTerm/Wayland clipboard provider layer.

Team research (4 teammates): Root cause confirmed as wl-copy focus stealing on GNOME Wayland + provider caching preventing OSC 52 override. Fix: set vim.g.clipboard early in options.lua before clipboard=unnamedplus.
Refined 4-module yank ring architecture (~235 LOC): ring.lua, highlight.lua, telescope.lua, init.lua + plugin spec. No clipboard.lua or recovery.lua needed.
Refined 3-phase plan: core modules, plugin spec wiring, reference cleanup. 2 hours estimated.
Implementation summary: 3 phases, yanky.nvim replaced with custom yank ring

memory_candidates.content: TeammateTool spawns in team-mode skills do NOT specify subagent_type, making them eligible for CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 cache sharing. Teammates 2-N inherit the parent's prompt cache prefix, reducing input token cost by ~90%. Both CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1 and CLAUDE_CODE_FORK_SUBAGENT=1 must be enabled. The actual cache benefit depends on the byte-identical shared prefix ratio -- prompts should put shared content first and teammate-specific assignments last to maximize overlap.

completed_projects.description: Design the unified workflow architecture based on research findings from task 591. Covers: (1) shared command infrastructure to eliminate ~900 lines of duplicated arg parsing, flag handling, extension routing, and GATE IN/OUT across /research, /plan, /implement. (2) Shared skill base pattern to deduplicate ~80% identical preflight/postflight across workflow skills. (3) /orchestrate state machine design: task state detection -> action selection -> agent dispatch -> handoff consumption -> loop. (4) Fork vs. subagent decision tree (fork decision matrix) for each workflow stage: forks for same-turn re-dispatch (blocker escalation, team mode), fresh subagents for sequential phases. (5) dispatch_agent() abstraction: single function encapsulating fork-vs-named-subagent decision, future-proofing against Anthropic 'named fork' API when it arrives. (6) Handoff protocol specification: structured handoff objects (200-400 tokens) replacing raw artifact injection (2000-5000 tokens). (7) Extension integration points for the new architecture, including lifecycle hook interface. (8) Nested loop resolution: orchestrator outer loop and implementer inner continuation loop must be exclusive alternatives, not nested layers.

memory_candidates.content: When shell scripts need to export variables into a calling shell, they must be sourced (not called as a subprocess). Claude Code command scripts sourcing shared library scripts (like parse-command-args.sh, command-gate-in.sh) must use source script.sh args within a single Bash tool invocation. Scripts that only produce side effects (gate-out, postflight) can use subprocess calls.

When extracting shared logic from command markdown files, multi-task dispatch blocks (~115 lines) are not extractable in the same pass as GATE IN/GATE OUT/parse sections — they have per-command differences. Extract them in a separate dedicated task.

(2) skill-meta SKILL.md: add pre-confirmation stage between context preparation and agent spawn -- skill proposes tasks, user selects/revises via AskUserQuestion, then passes confirmed list. (3) meta-builder-agent.md: add confirmed mode that accepts pre-validated task list and creates without re-asking; keep interactive mode for no-args /meta where agent runs foreground. (4) Update multi-task-creation-standard.md to note that confirmation must happen in foreground.

memory_candidates.content: AskUserQuestion called from background agents (spawned via Claude Code Task tool) does not reliably surface to the foreground user conversation. The prompt appears in the background agent's thread which the user cannot see. The established fix is to call AskUserQuestion in the skill layer (foreground) before spawning any subagent. Reference implementations: skill-fix-it, skill-learn. This is a platform limitation, not a configuration issue.

The pre-confirmation pattern for skills that delegate to agents: (1) skill does lightweight analysis and proposes task list, (2) skill calls AskUserQuestion for confirmation (foreground), (3) if confirmed, skill passes confirmed_tasks list to agent with mode=confirmed, (4) agent creates tasks without re-asking. This separates interactive confirmation (foreground) from mechanical task creation (background agent). Used to fix /meta prompt mode.

completed_projects.description: Update the context system for progressive disclosure and agent context budget caps. Four-tier loading model: Tier 1 (always, ~500L: anti-stop-patterns, return-metadata, checkpoint-execution), Tier 2 (command-specific, ~500L: routing tables, arg docs, anti-bypass), Tier 3 (agent-specific, ~3-5K lines: workflow patterns, domain context), Tier 4 (on-demand, unbounded: guides, templates, examples). Budget caps: sonnet workers <=8K tokens, opus planners <=15K tokens, haiku utilities <=2K tokens. Audit 97 context index entries for tier classification; prune dead entries. Commands MUST NOT load Tier 3 context (routing only). Move embedded agent context from commands to Tier 3 agent context files. This task is ELEVATED in dependency chain: context budget design must precede task 594 (skill base). Reference: .claude/docs/architecture/architecture-spec.md cross-cutting context section.

memory_candidates.content: Synthesis fork pattern for team research: The lead should NOT read all teammate output files inline (anti-pattern accumulating 4-12k tokens). Instead, fork an anonymous agent with the teammate file paths as @-references. The synthesis agent reads files in its own fresh context, performs conflict detection and gap analysis, writes the unified report, and returns a ~200-word summary. Lead context grows by ~250 tokens instead of 7-21k tokens.

When team skills (skill-team-research, skill-team-plan) need to synthesize outputs from multiple parallel teammates, dispatch a named synthesis-agent rather than having the lead read all files inline. The synthesis-agent runs in a fresh context, reads all teammate finding files as @-references, and returns a compact summary (~200 words) to the lead. This keeps lead context growth at ~900 tokens instead of 7-21k tokens. The synthesis-agent is at .claude/agents/synthesis-agent.md and has allowed-tools: Read, Write only.

completed_projects.description: Fix generate-task-order.sh to properly handle Task Order sections. Three sub-issues: (1) Completed tasks like #232 still appear in Task Order despite being terminal - verify the script properly excludes them. (2) The description field used in tree display may contain artifact paths instead of task descriptions - investigate why artifact-like content appears. (3) Ensure the script works correctly for projects with both auto-generated topic headings and hand-curated Track/Tier/Phase headings. The BimodalLogic project uses custom heading structure that diverges from the auto-generated format. Either (a) make the script preserve custom structure during regeneration, or (b) ensure projects configure state.json topics to produce equivalent auto-generated output. Also check that link-artifact-todo.sh is not incorrectly matching task numbers in the Task Order section (it uses grep for ### N. which should only match Tasks section, but verify).

completed_projects.description: Fix Task Order status sync and completed task pruning. completed_projects.description: Add multi-task argument support to /orchestrate command with dependency-aware execution ordering. (1) Update orchestrate.md STAGE 0 to use parse-command-args.sh for multi-task parsing with single-task fallthrough. (2) Add batch validation and dependency graph construction from state.json dependencies field. (3) Implement topological wave dispatch: tasks without inter-dependencies run in parallel via concurrent skill-orchestrate invocations; tasks with dependencies wait for predecessors to complete before launching. (4) Add consolidated output and batch commit for multi-task runs. (5) Update multi-task-operations.md with orchestrate-specific section covering dependency-aware dispatch vs pure-parallel. (6) Update CLAUDE.md command table to show new argument syntax (N[,N-N]).

Fixed command-gate-out.sh to handle orchestrate operations and corrected operator precedence bug. Changes applied to both canonical and extension mirror copies.

completed_projects.description: Refactor skill-orchestrate to support multi-task mode where a single orchestrator agent manages all tasks instead of spawning one agent per task. Add multi-task code path to SKILL.md state machine: single agent receives all task numbers and dependency graph, tracks per-task phase in a compact status table, dispatches phase-specific workers (research-agent, planner-agent, impl-agent) directly using wave-based dependency-aware scheduling. Reduces orchestration overhead from O(N * 44k) to O(1 * 44k) tokens. Also fix TODO.md synchronization: ensure the single orchestrator properly calls update-task-status.sh to sync status markers in TODO.md (not just state.json) and calls link-artifact-todo.sh after artifact creation to link reports/plans/summaries in TODO.md task entries.
